reviews.review_title: Excellent game - well worth the purchase
reviews.review_description:
I bought this game on the strength of the developers prior ’Room’ series of games, (which were excellent), and I was not disappointed in the least with ‘Ghost Town’.

The very excellent graphics and audio atmosphere are just the first things to be praised in this game.  The story is intriguing, the puzzles, (while not fiendish), are engaging, and the attention to details is fascinating.  (There are full articles in the newspapers that are just scattered on the floor, letters from the council pinned to the cork board, and if you get the chance, read the eviction notice for flat 91 - see if you notice what I did).

My only criticism of this game is that while I spend a good few hours over a couple of days playing the 23 chapters of the 7 sections of this game, I really wanted it to be longer; Or have a sequel ready to play!

I bought my first VR in 2022 and I started with Resident Evil 4. This was a bad move because it was so engaging that I ended up abandoning my VRs after not finding anything that could match up. Well, after a year of no contact, I downloaded this game after seeing it in an article and oh my goodness - it blew me away!! The story and setting are so immersive - the Occult museum in the Warlock's house will forever be one of my favourite VR moments. The only criticism I have is that the game should have gone on for another 20 hours! I have just gotten their other game, The Room, and cannot wait to see what else they will come up with!

I already played all the Room games from Fireproof Games and this one didn't disappoint either. Unlike the escape room style, this one takes you through a story spanning a number of years, set in eighties UK and the little details are great. There is incentive to play it again and again as there are places to explore and things to find. The puzzles aren't too taxing so it can be completed in a few hours. I'm looking forward to the sequel...

I am 59 years old and I love seated puzzle games. I have played this one twice now. I love this game. The puzzles aren't so hard you have to go look them up every time. Just keep trying and you'll get it. I sure hope they make more games like this one. This is even better than The Room!

reviews.review_title: Supernatural World I Don’t Want to Leave
reviews.review_description:
I love everything about Ghost Town.

This isn’t a game I want to rush through. I’ve been taking my time, slowly walking around, exploring every corner, and really soaking in all the little details the developers put into the world. The atmosphere is incredible and it pulls you in right away.

The supernatural/paranormal vibe is completely my thing. It feels mysterious, a little eerie, and immersive in the best way. I catch myself just standing there looking around because the environments are so well done.

Honestly, I’m already excited for a sequel and I haven’t even finished the game yet. That says a lot.

If you like slower, atmospheric exploration with a strong supernatural theme, this one’s absolutely worth your time.

One of the most amazing VR games I’ve ever experienced. The story is engaging, the graphics are stunning, and the riddles are thoughtfully designed and well-organized. Everything feels detailed and polished, making it easy to get fully immersed. I absolutely loved it.

reviews.review_title: The highest quality VR exclusive so far
reviews.review_description:
Where should I start?
First off, this is the best looking game I got to play in Quest 3. It has stunning visuals, not only in terms of cheer graphic fidelity but also atmosphere, with a wide variety of environments, both indoor and outdoor. This game made me feel like I was actually transported to a different place.
The characters are charming and make you care about the story, an area where most VR games tend to be lacking. While it definitely gets scary at times, I really appreciate the absence of jumpscares, relying instead on atmosphere.
The gameplay integrates seamlessly with the story,  rather than being puzzles just for the sake of puzzles, each one you encounter feel like an integral part of the story. It has great balance and pacing.
My only gripe is that the end doesn't really feel like a culmination, it just kinda ends all of a sudden.
The puzzles in this make you think, but they are not too hard! I love the graphics and the story so far is good. This is a good game to get!!

reviews.review_title: not worth 30 dollars worth to short
reviews.review_description: Love the game don't get me wrong.  Had me so immersed, I felt i was there and little spooked at certain areas.  The details so vivid I just loved it.  I did have so much fun.  But for 30 dollars I would have liked more.  The ending left me dissatisfied and longing.  That is why i gave it a 4.
What a brilliant game.  So easy to lose track of time when moving through this wonderful story. Can’t wait for the next episode.
This game felt so real, I didn’t want Edith’s journey to end. I would love to hear news that there will be continuation.
Incredible, atmospheric puzzler. Cant wait for a sequel. One of the best vr experiences you will play. Completely immersive, very little jank if any and a truly captivating story. Whats not to love

A great example all developers should follow.

Highly recommend!

Agree 100% with all the other great reviews.

Don’t sleep on this one.

reviews.review_title: Great graphics and pretty good puzzles
reviews.review_description: The graphics are impressive and the puzzles are fun and not too difficult (maybe a bit too easy). The story, like most such games, is rather lame and unfortunately you have to sit through long cut scenes without the ability to skip through them.  I really couldn't care less about the story line.  It's pretty fun, though, and I probably spent about 7 hours to go through it all.   3 1/2 stars

If you want a “cinematic experience” then this is a great game for you. The graphics and sound design are really good. But the gameplay is sparse. It felt like I was getting 30 minutes of puzzles for every 2 hours of story. Don’t expect much more than a walking simulator

Well deserved, Ghost Town is a great game.  Not just a VR game, but this beats many games on other platforms.

Get it, you won't be disappointed.

I’ll start with the positive: the graphics are very well designed and genuinely immersive. At times you can forget about the game entirely and just enjoy looking around. The puzzles are fine, the mechanics are decent, and overall the gameplay is pleasant.

Now for what I didn’t like. Above all, the gameplay is ridiculously short, especially compared to other games in this category such as The 7th Guest, The Room VR, Red Matter, and others. The developers probably knew this, because they seem to pad the experience with long, unskippable conversations, long walks between locations, and lengthy non-interactive scenes. If you focus on the essentials and avoid wandering around, you could probably finish the whole game in a single sitting. There are also very few objects to interact with, and the “extras” are limited to a handful of one-page magazines you can collect.
This is another classic. I enjoyed Ghost Town from start to finish. The puzzles were pitched just right and the story trotted on nicely. I loved the UK locations. More please 🙏

one of the top 5 game i have play
no need of a gun for having fun ...not scary but have a good vibes and you never know what be next...
nice graphique, nice story,well build and  so original
its that kind of game that give me hope for the future of vr
i really hope you made a second one...please make it fast  a cant wait for 
thanks to all the teams
nice jobs

I don’t have a lot of games because I can’t find many I like. 

I got hooked on their quality when I downloaded “Room VR: A Dark Matter”. The game is brilliant!

So, periodically, when I check for new games, I always first check to see if they have released anything. I was excited to find this game and it has not disappointed yet. Graphics and special effects are extraordinary, the story plot is engaging, and the puzzles are challenging, but not impossible. So far I’ve had to use a couple of hints and then slapped my forehead for not having seen the obvious.

I have two “disappointments” that I can live with and I’m only posting them as feedback for future games:

1. New customers can purchase both games as a package. As an existing, supporting customer, I had to spend $30 to simply purchase this single game despite already owning “Dark Matter”. I can live with it because these folks deserve to be compensated for such great work, but a “bolt on” price would have been more fair.

2. Progress is only saved after a significant event. That’s a big issue for me; especially in cases where I must pause because life gets in the way of playing OR the helmet requires charging. Hopefully that will be addressed in future updates and new games.

Great job! PLEASE release more similar games. You are a big fish in a small pond with your approach to this genre of games. Everyone else seems more focused on macabre, bloodsport, and violence. You challenge me mentally while providing a great and engaging story. Keep up the excellent work!

Loved this Game! Not one negative comment. So many trippy scenarios to work through. The puzzles were the right amount of challenging but not impossible. Graphics were great too and loved the characters. I counted 2 or 3 times when I thought the game was over and i was pleasantly wrong. lol! Wish I could already purchase the sequel on this one.
Loved it and want more!

I just finished this game and it was so much fun!  I loved that the puzzles were challenging but not frustrating and I really enjoyed to storyline!  I hope these developers come out with a similar game soon because I’d definitely buy it!

Go around solving mysteries in spooky locations to free ghosts from our mortal realm.  

10/10 puzzles, voice acting, music/graphics/style/ambience.  

Runs extremely well on Quest 3 with no stuttering, which is impressive considering how gorgeous the graphics are (best I’ve seen on standalone VR, better than Red Matter 2).

reviews.review_title: Not even sure how far through I am, but
reviews.review_description: I had to stop playing to give a round of applause to the creators. This game is so immersive it gives me what I want from a VR headset - to take me to another place for a while. The graphics, gameplay and especially the soundtrack hook you in an instant. Putting it down is all but impossible. The puzzles could be a little trickier, but apart from that, so far, everything is cracking.

I didn't finish the game yet, but I am very thrilled about the experience. Very good graphics, very good, but not too hard (to make you get stuck) puzzles and riddles. You have to pay attention to details. The story behind it, is a classic combination of occult, witchcraft, London, puzzles, mysteries and curiosity to find what is behind the next locked door. I am very happy, for me it is worth the money and I can't wait to see the next game from these developers out. Cheers guys!

reviews.review_title: A real gem for puzzle fans
reviews.review_description: I love horror, but have never been able to push through truly scary games, whether in VR or flat. Lighter fare like this is more my speed. I played it in October and it was nice to get those Spooktober vibes, without actually getting scared out of my mind. But the real appeal is the intuitive, never-too-grating puzzles and the excellent narrative. My only quibble is the length and how the game ends on a bit of a cliffhanger. I hope the developer is able to build upon their success with the first game to deliver a compelling conclusion to Edith's story.

Completed a few days ago and wow, what a ride, game was amazing with fun puzzles and a very interesting story that leaves you wanting more. Hopefully they make a sequel to expand on this amazing world and characters ☺️
The story and world drew me in and the puzzles were excellent.  The graphics were excellent and completely Immersive! Met my high expectations!
Best puzzle game, hoping for a part 2!

reviews.review_title: Fantastic - hope a sequel comes soon!
reviews.review_description: Absolutely loved this game. The story, the audio, the visuals, the puzzles. Cannot wait for a sequel!
Awesome VR game and I hope they continue the story!
Stunning Details, physics and more honestly one of the if not the best VR Games to date!

I am new to Vr and this was a perfect place to start. The visuals and sounds are great. The puzzles I think are easy if you pay attention. The only things I would change is there is a lot of walking around doing nothing and I wish it was longer. I think if you are new to Vr this game is a nice place to start.

reviews.review_title: This needs a museum, sequel requested
reviews.review_description: I needs a sequal ASAP, this game matches with half life alyx, hell even beats half life alyx from how good it is! i need a sequel or something thats like the game RIGHT NOW!

reviews.review_title: Like playing VR for the first time again
reviews.review_description: I love VR and have been playing for years, but naturally, that initial “wow” factor fades into the background. Playing this game, however, made me feel like I did the first time I put a headset on. I felt like I was there in that world and story with those characters and lost track of time. I was sad when it ended because I didn’t want to leave so soon. I hope there is more to come. I will be there first day for whatever the developer has planned next.

I finished the game. I thoroughly enjoyed this game. The graphics are great and the voice acting is very well done, the puzzles are a lot of fun, the story is well written. I didn't buy the game as I have horizon+ and it was one of the free catalogue games. For the price it's being sold for I think it's still worth playing as it was really enjoyable. I can understand some of the negative reviews not being happy with the length of the game and lack of interactable items aside from the main tasks and the magazines you can collect.l but still I don't feel that such a big deal granted how well done this game is. I hope they do a sequel.
Just the right amount of difficulty to keep it challenging and the story line was actually interesting.

Despite having been a consumer of VR since the Oculus Dev Kit many many years ago, I think this may be my first review. I felt compelled to take the time to share my gratitude to the devs, actors, musicians, artists and all involved with this magnificent game. The experience was just amazing from start to finish. The visuals, acting, sound design, story and game play were a perfect blend of intrigue, eeriness and challenges. 

I'm not a gamer. In fact, I'm probably a bit older than most here, but have been in tech for many years. I didn't grow up in the video game era, however. Many games I've tried in VR just ended up being overly complex with the challenges or puzzles. After trying the same thing over and over and failing, I'd usually just move on out of frustration. Ghost Town was an enjoyable experience throughout. The puzzles were challenging but logical and achievable. Most impressively, it felt like I was the protagonist throughout, living the story. It was extraordinarily immersive.

Bottom line is that I truly enjoyed every moment of this fantastic game. I hope it will not be the last from this talented team.

I had previously played Fireproof’s fantastic The Room VR: A Dark Matter game, but was hesitant with Ghost Town because the term means an ‘abandoned settlement’, and they last thing I wanted, as experienced in other games, is walking around an empty (haunted) environment solving puzzles. I don’t know why they used this irrelevant term for the game title (other than it contains ‘ghosts’), but rest assured it’s completely the opposite. This feels to me like an immersive well paced and engrossing movie with outstanding graphically rendered and interesting locations, containing characters with high quality motion capture, voice acting and dialogue (including the character you imbue – Edith). The puzzles and hint system are logical and a delight to play. If only all single player VR games could be this good.

I’ve played several vr puzzle games, and consider The Room: A Dark Matter to be the gold standard. Ghost Town is every bit as good, imo. Great graphics and environments. Puzzles are fun and challenging without being too hard. Good story that’s easy to follow. The end seemed to indicate that there will be a sequel. I look forward to that.
Love the graphics, i wish they could re program where i don't have to click the steps to walk. I want to just click once and walk,..
Good puzzler
Honestly a top tier puzzle game with hints if horror
Ever played a game and wished it would never end?  Loved the whole story but wanted it to be far longer. There really has to be a sequel. Please?
One of the most atmospheric standalone games on the quest. Graphics and performance that are almost PCVR worthy. A must buy!

This game has wonderful graphics, spooky atmosphere, and a good premise, But the scenes can't be explored fully, the game doesn't allow any freedom, especially when characters are speaking slow drawn out monologues that you aren't allowed to skip.  If you take a break right after listening to a character and close the game, then when you start back up, you must listen to the whole thing again.

Then there are the repetitive puzzles, like in the lighthouse, where I was forced to set a switch then walk around walls to set another on the other side.  Those puzzles were so frustrating! IT took 5 or more manipulations and the navigation of several walls to finish simple puzzles that took no logic to solve.

I wanted a refund but I had wasted too much time, in the lighthouse in frustration,. trying to get out of the lighthouse. So now I'm stuck with an expensive game that I hate and will never finish.

I have all of The Room games by this designer, and everyone was fantastic, so I trusted that Ghost Town would be my new favorite.. I can't figure out how to ask again for a refund, so I won't buy games from Fireproof again.  There should be more than a time limit making decisions on customer satisfaction and refunds.

I really enjoyed this game and how intuitive it is. It’s not like some where you have to go online to find a walk-through or hope to God you stumble upon something by the chance lol.

I’m really hoping there’s going to be a second part to this because of the way it ended 

￼

The graphic and sound effects are good. I love the story too. However, it's too straightforward and the puzzles are too simple. No decision you need to make, not many things you can explore or interact with, and not scary at all. It could be more interesting if it's more scary and challenging.

reviews.review_title: Can't see anything, way too dark
reviews.review_description: It starts out fairly good, but once you get inside the lighthouse  it is way too dark to see anything, even using the flashlight and with the brightness turned all the way up on the headset.

The game was amazing. The interactions in vr are very smooth and accurate. The characters were fantastic. The environments where intense and well detailed. The puzzle elements where good maybe a bit to simple. I found "The Room" puzzles more elaborate and engaging. The flow of this game was amazing and if you had the battery life you could prolly finish it in 3 sessions. I think my playthrough was 8.5 hours. 
Another amazing game and hope we get a sequel.

This is a must experience title for standalone. The environments are absolutely stunning and the horror aspects of this game are some of the best imaginable. I have been using standalone vr for about 5 years now and this game is unlike anything I’ve ever experienced. Hats off to the devs for making this even creepier than I expected. I love horror movies and psychological thrillers and I am amazed at what I’m experiencing here.

reviews.review_title: How a vr game should be
reviews.review_description: Amazing, bu unfortunately short

It was a fun story and with fun puzzles. The puzzles made you think, but weren't difficult. I like games with shorter run times, so the pacing and length was just right for me. While not the biggest fan of these things, I do have friends who are sensitive to demonic/witchcraft things, so if that's you then this might not be the game for you. For me, I'll be interested in a sequel

I have eagerly awaited each new game launched by FireProofGames since The Room. They are so much fun. Sadly Ghost Town sucks! Wandering around doing nothing for half the game. Terrible ending. I can’t believe how bad this is.

As to little surprise with all the other praise already given, I also very much enjoyed this game. The details in the puzzles are great. The world is intriguing and sometimes spooky. Game doesn’t overstay its welcome.

reviews.review_title: Easily the best game of its genre in VR!
reviews.review_description: I absolutely enjoyed every second of this game. The puzzles are fun and challenging and the game saves often so unlike most people, I did a small part every day and the experience lasted. It was like taking part in an adventure book with a huge difference…you are the main character! No more just turning pages in a story before bed, it’s time to get involved in the action. I can’t praise the experience enough. Obviously a lot of love and attention to detail and creative genius went into crafting this absolute masterpiece and it really shows. I was continually surprised and amazed. The atmosphere of the game was spooky and powerful . It felt like there was a lot of thought behind the story and essentially the game felt like it had an entire history behind it and it was a fully established story universe of its own. I was sad when I had completed the game after a week of playing each evening before bed (it gave me some interesting dreams!) but excited at the hints of a sequel which I will be eagerly awaiting.
Good puzzles and great atmosphere make this one a winner. If you liked The Room, you’ll love this. More please!
Incredible immersive design, great story, and good puzzles. It's a must play!

This is one of the best VR games ever, the graphics are top notch even on standalone VR headset, great story & smooth game play, no jank as is often the case with VR games, great ghost mystery story all round, must play ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Great game, very well made. The graphics are quite good for a Meta Quest title. The puzzles aren’t overly difficult, which is appreciated. It’s very immersive overall, although there are a few things that could be improved. The dialogues are extremely long and can’t be skipped. You also 
can’t run — I assume this is to prevent motion sickness — but in many levels you have to walk long distances and it feels very slow. Another thing I don’t like is that when you interact with certain characters, the game places physical barriers so you can’t get close to them, which slightly breaks immersion. It would also be nice to be able to interact with more objects in the environments; for example, when you’re in Edith’s house, you can barely touch anything. Looking forward for part two.
Would definitely recommend!

I very rarely write reviews but I just had to with this one. WOW! This is one of the best games I have ever played. Puzzles, great story, awesome graphics all make this a perfect experience. It obviously depends what type of games you like as always, but if you are into escape room type puzzles this is for you. The puzzles are pitched just right, not too hard and not too easy and there are handy hints always available should you get stuck. My advice, go get it now, you won't be disappointed.

Saw this game while looking for a good horror game to play and came across Ghost Town here. I started playing it last night and holy crap it didn’t disappoint! 
The atmosphere was absolutely amazing and very well made. I also love the story line and all the puzzles as well. I’m glad to say it’s definitely worth the 25$!

I’m really impressed with this game! Great story, great graphics, the puzzles are just perfect, not too difficult, not too easy.. I have tons of games and have not beaten any of them. This will be the first game I finish😁.

great to have this option but i was so connected to the island continue when its timed out, I buyed directly    so great graphics on my quest 2 is't somewhat a guide play and now i just have todo a simple job replacing a light bulk can't be that difficult or not ....
Great puzzles. I loved this game at least as much as The Room.
Just finished Ghost Town and I had so much fun playing! It has everything! Can't wait for GT2! Great job, dev team!
Definitely one of the best games I've played it's got me hooked everything about this game is brilliant. 10/10 please make another one
This game is incredible especially on the Meta Quest 3S.  Graphics are unreal for a standalone VR experience.  The storyline is gripping.  Looking forward to hearing about a sequel. 😀
Great graphics, puzzles and story. Smooth play, no crashing. My only complaint is that it wasn’t long enough. Also, as a female who loves these type of games, it was nice to embody a female.

I was thoroughly immersed in the world and characters. Amused by her judgement of the father when I thought he was cool and she was a brat by comparison. She went above and beyond for her brother, went through a lot for his dumb decisions. Well, I’d be there if a sequel is made. Throughly enjoyed everything.
This game looks and plays so well and has a great story line. It isn't all about the jump scares or bloody gore, it's a good puzzler and heroics game.  I hope they make more to this game.

reviews.review_title: AAA in my experience, loved it!!
reviews.review_description:
I would just like to say I've enjoyed every second of Ghost Town and look forward to more ghostly investigations with Miss Edith. 
I absolutely loved it!
Guys please make another one! I absolutely loved ghost town! Graphics were great, story was great, the puzzles were great fun!

This game's story is tender and makes me tear up. The game is also lovingly put together, mechanically and visually speaking. This game is NOT scary, just atmospheric, so please give it a go if you do not usually play horror games. I'll be looking out for these devs' next game!

I must have about a dozen games that I am literally stuck on and cannot finish due to the difficulty. So nice to have a game with beautiful graphics,  easy interactions, and puzzles that give hints and aren't impossible to solve. I highly recommend this experience.
That was fun, looking forward to the next chapter!
Just a great game! I would play it for the incredibly immersive and convincing scenes alone. The gameplay, puzzles and story are equally incredible.

Coming from the same developer behind the classic puzzle game "The Room" I was looking forward a challenging time but unfortunately nearly all the puzzles were solved quickly.

The graphics are generally excellent, some of the environments are among the best I've experienced in VR on any platform.  Sometimes it feels like you're in a high quality CGI movie.  This aspect of the game alone was more than worth the price of admission for me. I got it on sale, but I'd still be very happy with it even at full price.

However there are a few areas where the visuals could use another Quest 3 update.

The flashlight effect is very basic and looks out of place, it would have greatly added to the atmosphere if it cast shadows. In an update they added dynamic character shadows to the Quest 3 version which makes the lack of flashlight shadows even more jarring.

A few of the environments are a little underwhelming visually and unfortunately the game starts in one of those and that makes for a disappointing first impression.

Immersion would benefit from more hand presence. Your hand passes through most objects like you're a ghost.

I still had a great time just following the story and seeing all the sights.  Even the easiest puzzles are satisfying to solve because of the beautifully designed objects you interact with. I replayed some levels to hunt down all the collectables and I enjoyed being in the environments again.

If you're looking for a high challenge and you don't place a premium on art direction or atmosphere you may want to skip this.
